Environmental factors doesn't necessarily mean social environment, e.g how you were raised. The womb is developmentally the most important environment. So it's not just genes, but you're literally born that way because the level of testosterone and estrogen in the womb during certain phases shaped how parts of your brain regulating sexual attraction (e.g hypothalamus) developed. Also, epigenetics (=the activation or deactivation of genes) is also driven by environmental factors. So if you have genes that make you likely to be straight, but these genes deactivate (so they basically stop doing anything) due to environmental triggers while your brain is still in early development, you are more likely to be gay.
